max_events_per_user.name: Max events in context
max_events_per_user.hint: Maximum number of recent events injected into LLM context per user

max_profiles_per_user.name: Max profile entries
max_profiles_per_user.hint: Maximum number of profile key-value pairs allowed per user

max_event_keep.name: Max events stored
max_event_keep.hint: Maximum number of event logs kept in database per user (oldest auto-deleted)

skills_dir.name: Skills directory
skills_dir.hint: Path to skills directory (defaults to data/skills/). Each skill is a subfolder containing either SKILL.md (recommended, single-file YAML frontmatter) or the legacy manifest.json + instruction.md pair.

max_context_chars.name: Max context chars per user
max_context_chars.hint: Maximum characters of memory context injected per user (0 = unlimited). Truncates by category priority when exceeded

inject_categories.name: Auto-injected categories
inject_categories.hint: Profile categories pushed into the system prompt every turn. Other categories stay queryable via memory_query(category=...). Use ["*"] to inject every category (legacy behaviour). Default: ["basic"]

disabled_skills.name: Disabled skills
disabled_skills.hint: List of skill names to disable (won't be registered as LLM tools). Example: ["tarot_reading", "daily_fortune"]

enable_slash_commands.name: Enable slash commands
enable_slash_commands.hint: Allow users to trigger skills via /command (e.g. /tarot, /fortune). When disabled, skills can only be triggered by LLM.

webui_port.name: Memory WebUI port
webui_port.hint: Port for the memory management WebUI (0 = disabled, valid range: 1-65535). Example: 8765

webui_host.name: Memory WebUI host
webui_host.hint: Bind address for the memory WebUI. Use 127.0.0.1 for local-only access

webui_token.name: Memory WebUI token
webui_token.hint: Access token for the memory WebUI (leave empty for no authentication, only safe for local access)

memory_auditor_enabled.name: Memory auditor (active recall)
memory_auditor_enabled.hint: Run a separate fast-LLM call after each turn to extract any facts the main LLM missed. Costs one extra LLM call per user batch. Default off — enable if your main LLM is too conservative about calling memory_update. ⚠️ Privacy: enabling this sends the user's latest message AND up to 50 of their existing profile entries (nicknames, locations, relationships, etc.) to the auditor model — which may be a different vendor than your main LLM if you set memory_auditor_model_uuid. Make sure that vendor's data-handling policy is acceptable before turning this on.

memory_auditor_model_uuid.name: Memory auditor model
memory_auditor_model_uuid.hint: Leave empty (recommended) to use the configured default fast LLM. Pick a specific model only when you want to pin the auditor to it.

memory_auditor_skip_keywords.name: Memory auditor skip keywords
memory_auditor_skip_keywords.hint: If the user's latest message contains any of these, the auditor skips that turn entirely (privacy guard for messages that shouldn't be recorded).

memory_auditor_max_inflight.name: Memory auditor max concurrent calls
memory_auditor_max_inflight.hint: Maximum number of concurrent in-flight auditor LLM calls. When this many audits are already running, new turns are dropped (best-effort; auditor is an opt-in bonus layer). Lower = stricter, fewer rate-limit hits; higher = more thorough but more provider load.

enable_fts5.name: Enable FTS5 full-text search
enable_fts5.hint: Create a SQLite FTS5 virtual table over event_logs so memory_query(query=...) can do BM25 ranking instead of substring matching. Zero new dependencies (FTS5 is built into Python's bundled sqlite3 on every supported platform). Disable only if your SQLite build lacks FTS5 or you don't want the per-write trigger overhead — falls back to LIKE search.

enable_embedding.name: Enable semantic embedding recall
enable_embedding.hint: Add a cosine-similarity rerank stage to memory_query(query=...). Off by default — needs either an LLM client that exposes an embed/aembed method OR a local sentence-transformers install (~480MB). When unavailable, the pipeline silently falls back to FTS5/LIKE results without erroring.

enable_llm_rerank.name: Enable LLM rerank of recall results
enable_llm_rerank.hint: Add a fast-LLM precision pass after FTS5/embedding ranking. Costs one extra LLM call per memory_query(query=...) invocation. ⚠️ Same privacy note as the auditor: the user's query and up to 20 candidate event summaries are sent to the rerank model. Skip this when latency or cost matters more than precision.

memory_reranker_model_uuid.name: Memory reranker model
memory_reranker_model_uuid.hint: Leave empty (recommended) to reuse the auditor's model chain (configured uuid → fast LLM → default). Pin a specific model only when you want the reranker isolated from the auditor.

enable_query_aware_inject.name: Query-aware context injection (experimental)
enable_query_aware_inject.hint: When enabled, the most recent user message is used as a query to pull relevant events into the system prompt instead of (or alongside) the default 'last-N' window. Saves tokens but the LLM may not see context that wasn't keyword-related to the latest turn. Off by default — Phase 2 wires the recaller, Phase 3 will activate this fully.

enable_fact_dedup.name: Deduplicate restated events
enable_fact_dedup.hint: Compute a content hash for each memory_update(op='event') write. Restatements of the same fact (modulo case, whitespace, and filler particles) bump the existing row's importance + emit a reinforcement signal instead of inserting a duplicate. Disable to keep every event_logs write verbatim (legacy v2.0.0 behaviour).

evidence_half_life_days_rein.name: Evidence reinforcement half-life (days)
evidence_half_life_days_rein.hint: How fast reinforcement decays when no fresh signals arrive. After this many days an unreinforced fact's effective rein weight halves. Higher = stickier memory; lower = more forgiving of past patterns. Independent from the disp half-life (RFC §3.1.1).

evidence_half_life_days_disp.name: Evidence disputation half-life (days)
evidence_half_life_days_disp.hint: How fast a disputation signal decays. Shorter than rein by default — a user's objection should fade once they stop reasserting it, but a single 'I love cats' shouldn't need constant reaffirmation.

evidence_promoted_threshold.name: Evidence threshold: promoted
evidence_promoted_threshold.hint: Net evidence score (rein − disp, post-decay) at which a reflection is eligible for auto-promote to persona. Phase 3b uses this; Phase 3a stores the value but doesn't yet act on it.

evidence_confirmed_threshold.name: Evidence threshold: confirmed
evidence_confirmed_threshold.hint: Score above which a reflection counts as 'confirmed' (visible in the WebUI as a promoted-eligible candidate).

evidence_archive_threshold.name: Evidence threshold: archive candidate
evidence_archive_threshold.hint: Score at or below which a reflection is archive-eligible. Negative because net disputation has overwhelmed reinforcement; Phase 3b's reconciler will eventually move these out of active memory.

enable_reflection_synthesis.name: Enable reflection synthesis (Stage 2)
enable_reflection_synthesis.hint: After each turn, if the user has accumulated enough unabsorbed atomic facts, run a fast LLM call to synthesize one-sentence reflections (e.g. 'user has been running regularly') and store them as Tier-2 pending records. ⚠️ Adds one extra LLM call per eligible turn — same privacy considerations as the auditor: the unabsorbed event_logs rows are sent to the synthesizer.

enable_auto_promotion.name: Enable auto-promotion to persona (Stage 3)
enable_auto_promotion.hint: Start a background tick (default every 30 minutes) that scans pending reflections older than the configured age threshold. Reflections with no disputation signal and net evidence above the 'confirmed' threshold are auto-upserted into user_profiles with source='reflection_promote'. The WebUI lets you revoke any promotion with a single click.

reflection_min_facts.name: Reflection synthesis: min unabsorbed facts
reflection_min_facts.hint: How many unabsorbed atomic facts a user must accumulate before synthesis fires for them. Lower = faster reflections but more LLM cost; higher = denser groundings per reflection. Hard floor of 2 because reflections must be supported by ≥2 facts to be persisted.

reflection_promote_age_days.name: Reflection promote age (days)
reflection_promote_age_days.hint: Minimum age (since creation) for a pending reflection to be eligible for auto-promotion. Three days lets the user encounter and potentially dispute a new claim before it becomes part of their persona. Set to 0.0 for immediate eligibility (still gated by evidence threshold).

auto_promote_tick_seconds.name: Auto-promotion tick interval (seconds)
auto_promote_tick_seconds.hint: How often the auto-promotion loop sweeps for eligible reflections. 1800s (30 min) is the default — short enough to feel responsive, long enough to avoid hammering SQLite when many users have backlogs. Lower bound 60s; upper bound 24h.

enable_anti_repeat.name: Enable anti-repeat hint
enable_anti_repeat.hint: Before each LLM call, scan the recent assistant outputs for tokens (words / CJK trigrams) used across many of them and inject a one-line system hint asking the model to vary its phrasing. The corpus is in-memory only (lost on restart, no persistence overhead) and rebuilds within a few turns.

anti_repeat_lookback_turns.name: Anti-repeat: lookback turns
anti_repeat_lookback_turns.hint: How many of the user's most recent assistant outputs are scanned for over-used tokens. Higher = catches longer-running patterns but slower to react to a deliberate change in style. 10 is the sweet spot for typical chat.

anti_repeat_df_threshold.name: Anti-repeat: document-frequency threshold
anti_repeat_df_threshold.hint: A token must appear in at least this many of the lookback outputs to be flagged as over-used. Default 5 (half of the default lookback) catches genuinely repetitive patterns without flagging incidental shared vocabulary.
